author: @Gwydion67
text:
Sigh. The first time in years I had to pause for some months, and for sure I missed this whole competition. 😥
But hey, all those brilliant entries are still online, so I just watched as many of them as I could without blowing up my head. So many fine examples, so much inspiration. 💡😍👌
Guyes, you rock! And on top Rob as host. 😃👍

I hope to continue with my idea until I have a working version of sorts.

author: @aspudkicker
text: The submissions have given me ideas. The competition was a novel idea to gain ideas. The only idea for comp rules in the future would be for the entrants to say what they are going to use the prize for. Othewise Ive really enjoyed watching the videos and ideas.

author: @stuffoflardohfortheloveof
text: Just finished watching all of them. Watched in numerical order to save forgetting how far I’d got (took me three daily sessions as she wasn’t amused 😂). My scoring system is of no use to you at all as I’ve given everyone a thumbs up, simply because they were of interest and made the effort to do something (more than I did). There were some humorous ones, which I did prefer as a few made me laugh out loud, but they all deserve my vote anyway. I do hope you repeat this sometime as, if nothing else, it proved to me that you don’t have to be perfect, worried about your abilities, love the camera or have all the proper tools at your disposal……..it may even get me off my backside to do something. Well done Rob and thanks for the entertainment to everyone who entered. 👍

author: @alaingrignon
text: Hey everyone, and specifically all participants! WELL DONE EVERYONE! I've finally had a chance to go through all the videos. There are some really impressive ones in there and so many ideas to explore! I certainly had a blast building the CountR StaR (Counter Rotating Stator and Rotor)! Learned so much while doing it, had more than a few head-scratchers to deal with along the way too.. Just wish I would have had the time and proper materials to complete the Wind enabled version. I'm already looking at converting the planetary gears to Magnetic gears to reduce friction (Rob put up a video on this a while ago, just found it recently). I think that if I can stabilize the top platform and small bike wheels to stay straight, it will work. The other thing I wasn't able to mention in the video what that I used a lathe to take off some of the metal at the ends of the stator coils so as to reduce the magnetic pull. Finally, I hope that astute among you were able to notice my channeling my "Inner Rob" with my blue overall! LOL  Regardless of who gets the printer, I think everyone who participated is a winner! By what you've learned, By what you've accomplished, and just simply by "giving it a go!".

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Mate -it was great to see your entry and just to see you working away on something - loved the device by the way - thanks a million for 'giving it a go'

author: @steve29836
text: I'm the Magical Poppy guy. Can I just say Wow. I never expected to see so many entries. I entered because I thought not many would. The underlying message of my video was you don't need fancy tools and materials to just give it a go. I needn't have bothered! What we see from these videos is evidence of people young, old and from all over the world not just giving it a go but doing amazing things. Rob you should be proud of acheiving popularity across such a broad spectrum. To everyone that entered well done, regardless of comments, view counts (the youtube algorithm seems to have its own favourites!), likes or dislikes just getting off your bum and doing something gives you a badge of honour.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @GuardianEngraving
text: Hi, Love the work and all the videos. One concern I have (and this may be an invalid one :)) some of the videos have poor sound as in volume. I found it hard to adjust some to understand properly. I understand that this is the original way they were sent in and should stand on those grounds. My argument would be that to truly stand on equal footing within the competition the volumes should be normalised across the lot. As I fear some will suffer in votes due to such a simple jarring. I do believe you can re upload videos without altering their totals. Cheers

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering ah awesome thankyou, was not sure, but that seemed I was mishearing and it was that, but I wanted to ask anyway XD (also seems like PET might be useful in this arena since it absorbs water, so I am wondering if fully dried out, if it would absorb salts too that have been dissolved in it to make it an electrolyte it is absorbing XD)

*battling that moisture in it in 2 liter soda bottles to make single cell solar cell encapsulators, that can be strung together to make a roll out solar panel that the flexibility will absorb and deflect say hail ice balls to avoid damaging the cells inside, as the first test run I did actually worked out quite well, Let me know if you want some photos of the steps and the rig I made should you want to do a video on it. I will say I used C channel aluminum and nuts and all thread to make it, tried to make ribs from wrapping the c-channel but it was not really stronger walls and made if ridiculously difficult to remove the jig LOL**

unfortunately, I watched these videos in reverse order and thus THIS video is the one I saw last.  I did put likes on about half of them not knowing that this was counting towards your final score.
That being said - there was one that stood out to me which produced several new ideas in my own mind ( likely not the intention of his video ) and that was of the vibration pendulum in CO1-14.
In combination with other projects, I can see great potential here approaching perpetual motion similar to the pendulum powered by the earth's rotation in the Smithsonian Institute.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I don't mean to be rude here mate - so I apologise if I come across that way - but to be honest it seems a bit narrow minded to me to ask that - the answer to my mind is of course there is something special about it - just like there is something special about all people and effort that people make has to be applauded - I had a quick look at your channel - there's not a lot on it is there? Maybe if you did a few more of your own videos you might have a better understanding of what it takes in effort and personal risk to open yourself to the public who sometimes just don't think much beyond themselves - the idea of voting for something 'new to you' is of course you choice and as valid a reason as any for voting - but it is a little self centred in my opinion - please do be kinder to the folks who have taken part - at least in your publicly posted opinion - cheers and all the best

author: @scotttovey
text:
It's not that hard to police oneself.

I make it a point to type everything in a text editor for three reasons.

1) I can't see the small text that most social media sites set their text boxes too.
2) It gives me a chance to read through what I've typed. If I had a knee jerk reaction to something; I can easily edit that out and make my knee jerk side chill out.
3) It allows me to save a record of everything I post so that no one can come back later on and accuse me of saying something I did not say.

That to me is a win, win, win.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are right mate it isn't and most folks do a jimmy dandy job at it - but, unfortunately, as you know,  there is always a few - sigh lol

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
So true.
